LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DIDIER BOONEN'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES IS GRANTED IN PART. MR. BOONEN IS AWARDED FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $7,500, CALCULATED AS THIRTY HOURS AT AN HOURLY RATE OF $250. MR. BOONEN IS ENTITLED TO FEES AS THE PREVAILING PARTY, NOTWITHSTANDING HIS LIMITED SUCCESS. HIS MOTION IS NEITHER UNTIMELY NOR BARRED BY THE ENDORSEMENT ON THE COSTS CHECK. THE FEES AWARD IS DETERMINED ON A LODESTAR BASED ON REASONABLE HOURS AND REASONABLE HOURLY RATE. THIRTY HOURS IS REASONABLE AMOUNT OF TIME FOR THE WORK ON THIS CASE OTHER THAN THE POST-SETTLEMENT MOTIONS, WHICH ARE NOT COMPENSABLE. $250 IS A REASONABLE HOURLY RATE FOR MR. BOONEN'S COUNSEL. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-07-2017 Vacated

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jun-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF DIDIER BOONEN'S Motion For Attorneys Fees is GRANTED in part. Mr. Boonen is awarded fees in the amount of $7,500, calculated as thirty hours at an hourly rate of $250. Mr. Boonen is entitled to fees as the prevailing party, notwithstanding his limited success. His motion is neither untimely nor barred by the endorsement on the costs check. The fees award is determined on a lodestar based on reasonable hours and reasonable hourly rate. Thirty hours is reasonable amount of time for the work on this case other than the post-settlement motions, which are not compensable. $250 is a reasonable hourly rate for Mr. Boonen's counsel. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Clarify Or In The Alternative, Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-26-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Didier Boonen's motion to clarify judgment or, in the alternative, vacate judgment is denied in its entirety. The sole evidentiary basis for the motion is the declaration of Sally Nguyen. That declaration does not provide any evidentiary support for the apparent position of Mr. Boonen that Ms. Nguyen mistakenly believed that her signing defendants' 998 offer on February 1, 2017 resolved less than the entirety of the case. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-23-2016 Set For Trial On Jul-10-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
